meta.title: SBTI Personality Test
meta.subtitle: MBTI is outdated. SBTI is here.
meta.disclaimer: This test is for entertainment only. Don't use it as a diagnosis, job interview, matchmaking, breakup reason, fortune-telling, or life verdict.

ui.seoTestDescription: Take the viral Chinese SBTI personality test. 30 questions, 27 types including the hidden DRUNK and HHHH. Instant result with traits, compatibility, and the chengyu that matches your type.
ui.seoHubTitle: SBTI Personality Test — All 27 Types Explained
ui.seoHubDescription: Silly Behavioral Type Indicator — the viral Chinese parody of MBTI. 27 types, 15 dimensions, 30 questions, with the Chinese idioms (chengyu) that match each type.
ui.hubAllTypesSub: Every type paired with matching chengyu.
ui.hubSpecialTypesSub: HHHH is the fallback. DRUNK is an Easter egg only unlocked by a specific answer.
ui.hubCtaBullets: ~5 minutes · Instant result · Free · no signup
ui.hiddenInsideDesc: Two of the 27 types — HHHH and DRUNK — are Easter eggs. Only a specific pattern of answers will unlock them.
ui.socialProof: Viral on Weibo, Xiaohongshu, and TikTok in April 2026.
ui.errorState: Something went sideways. Let's start over.

result.hiddenTypeDesc: Triggered by the "baijiu in a thermos" answer — only ~1% of takers commit to this one.
result.brokeModelDesc: Your answers don't cleanly match any of the 25 standard types (best match under 60%).
result.normalBestTemplate: Your normal best match would have been {code}.
result.noResultDesc: This is the profile page for {code}. Take the test to see if this matches your own result.
result.profileSub: Across 5 models · Self, Emotion, Attitude, Action, Social

levels.L: You'd doubt a compliment before you'd trust it — rougher on yourself than anyone else is.
levels.M: Confidence moves with the weather. Tailwinds and you fly; headwinds and you fold.
levels.H: You roughly know your own worth, and a stranger's drive-by comment doesn't scatter it.

levels.L: Inner channel is mostly static. You loop on 'who am I actually' more than you'd like to admit.
levels.M: You mostly recognize yourself, though your mood sometimes borrows the account.
levels.H: You know your temper, your wants, and your hard nos.

levels.L: Comfort and safety matter more. Life doesn't need to be on sprint mode every day.
levels.M: Part of you wants to climb; part wants to lie down. The two negotiate constantly.
levels.H: You're easily pulled forward by a goal, a growth path, or something you believe in.

levels.L: An unread message can trigger a full mental movie about how it ends. Alarm system stays hot.
levels.M: Half trust, half test. You run internal war rooms over small things.
levels.H: You trust the relationship itself. A little wind doesn't blow the house down.

levels.L: You hold back. The door isn't closed — the security check is just thorough.
levels.M: You'll invest, but you keep a back pocket. No full send.
levels.H: Once you're in, you're in — time, energy, focus, all of it.

levels.L: You'll cling and you'll be clung to. Relationship warmth matters a lot.
levels.M: Both closeness and independence — adjustable, case-by-case.
levels.H: You need your own space. Even with love, some ground stays yours alone.

levels.L: Default filter is suspicion. Doubt first, approach later.
levels.M: Not naïve, not a full conspiracy theorist. Observing is your natural mode.
levels.H: You lean toward believing people mean well until proven otherwise.

levels.L: Rules are optional when comfort and freedom aren't.
levels.M: You'll follow the rule that matters. You'll bend the one that doesn't.
levels.H: Order feels right. You'd rather run the process than improvise.

levels.L: Meaning runs low. A lot of things feel like you're just going through the motions.
levels.M: Some days you have a mission, some days you're just vibing. The philosophy is half-booted.
levels.H: You roughly know which direction you're walking, and why.

levels.L: Risk-avoidance fires before ambition does. 'Don't crash' beats 'win'.
levels.M: Sometimes you want to win; sometimes you just want to not be bothered.
levels.H: Results, progress, forward motion — that's what lights you up.

levels.L: Your internal committee runs long. Every decision gets revisited.
levels.M: You'll deliberate, but you don't hang. Normal hesitation.
levels.H: You decide fast and don't re-litigate. Next.

levels.L: You and deadlines have a deep relationship. The later it gets, the more you wake up.
levels.M: You can do the work, but state varies. Sometimes consistent, sometimes coasting.
levels.H: Unfinished things feel like splinters. You push until they're done.

levels.L: Slow warmer. 'Making the first move' takes a whole inner pep talk.
levels.M: You'll engage if someone engages. You don't force it.
levels.H: You'll open the room. Standing out in a crowd doesn't rattle you.

levels.L: You prefer closeness and fusion. Once someone's in, they're in the inner circle fast.
levels.M: You want closeness and a little gap. Depends on the person.
levels.H: Your boundaries are real. Too-close triggers an instinctive step back.

levels.L: You're direct. What's in the head goes out the mouth, basically unfiltered.
levels.M: You read the room. Authenticity and politeness get dosed as needed.
levels.H: You're fluent at code-switching. The 'real you' gets released in layers.

main.text: I am a rat in the dark, a crawling cockroach. I have never been in love. I am timid and insecure. My youth is one daydream after another — imagining someone who loves me, wandering the streets with me, hanging out with me. Reality is: I spent my parents' money on school, I graduated, I grind a job, I chase 'goals.' Every time I see other people making happy jokes online, I want to cry. I envy the little good things in life and I'm still looking for my direction. Please, let those of us who lurk under the covers have a way out. I really don't want to soak a pillow in tears again.
I'm not good enough. Everyone around me is more capable than me.
I have a clear sense of who I really am.
There's something I genuinely want from life.
I need to keep climbing and keep getting better.
Strangers' opinions of me really don't matter to me.
Your partner hasn't replied to you in over 5 hours. They eventually say they 'had diarrhea the whole time.' Your honest reaction?

options.label: Diarrhea can't take 5 hours. They're hiding something.
Stuck somewhere between trust and doubt.
Maybe they really weren't feeling well today.

main.text: I often worry about being abandoned in relationships.
Hand on heart — I take every relationship I'm in seriously.

Your partner is: kind to elders, loving with children, gentle, honorable, articulate, witty, observant, widely-read, patient with beginners, friendly, humble, ambitious, radiant, devastatingly attractive, nation-toppling-level beautiful. What happens to you?

options.label: No matter how perfect they are, I won't fall too deep.
I'll treasure them completely. Yes, I will become an absolute fool in love.

main.text: You're dating someone very clingy. Your take?

options.label: I prefer keeping my own space.

main.text: I value personal space in every relationship.

options.label: I prefer depending and being depended on.

main.text: Most people are good.

options.label: Honestly? There's more evil in the human heart than there are hemorrhoids in the world.
Yes, I choose to believe good people outnumber the bad.

main.text: You're walking down the street. An adorable little girl (cute from every angle, cute on any phone camera, genuinely cute) skips up to you and hands you a lollipop. What do you feel?

options.label: Awwww she's so sweet and cute! She GAVE me a lollipop!
This might be some new kind of scam? Walk away, just in case.

main.text: Finals are coming. School mandates evening study sessions; skipping costs points. Tonight, though, your crush invited you to play your favorite competitive online game. What do you do?

options.label: Finals are literally coming, I'm not going anywhere.

main.text: I like breaking routines and I don't like being constrained.
I usually do things with a goal in mind.

One day it hits you: what real meaning does life have, anyway? In some sense, humans are just animals driven by urges, controlled by hormones — hungry so you eat, tired so you sleep. Just a dull, low-grade life form. Essentially no different from cats and dogs.
I do things mainly to get results and grow, not to avoid trouble and risk.
You've been sitting on the toilet for 30 minutes, constipated, nothing happening. You are most like:

options.label: Sit another 30 minutes. It might come.
Slap your own butt and say 'come on, damn it, out!'
Go get a laxative. Just get it over with.

main.text: I decide decisively. I don't like to hesitate.
This question has no question. Please guess blindly.

options.label: I thought really hard and I think I should pick A?

main.text: When people say 'you have strong execution,' your honest inner response is:

options.label: When I'm backed into a corner, yeah, my execution is SUPER strong…
Yes. Things are supposed to get done.

main.text: I usually plan things, ____

options.label: sometimes I finish them, sometimes not.
and I hate when my plan gets disrupted.

main.text: You've made a lot of online friends through a game you love. They invite you to meet up in person. Your move?

options.label: Talking online is fine — meeting IRL makes me nervous.
Sure, meeting is fine. Whoever talks to me, I'll talk back.
I'll dress up and chat enthusiastically. You never know. I mean, you NEVER KNOW.

main.text: Your friend brings along their friend. Your most likely state?

options.label: I feel instinctive distance from 'friend of friend.' Don't want to mess up the two-person dynamic.
Depends on them. If we click, we click.
A friend's friend counts as my friend! Let's go — enthusiastic chat mode.

main.text: My social vibe is basically an electric fence. Get too close and the alarm goes off automatically.
I crave closeness with people I trust — the kind of closeness where we feel like long-lost family.
Sometimes you have a different or negative opinion about something, but you don't voice it. The main reason is usually:

options.label: To preserve face or the relationship.
I don't want people to know I have a dark side.

main.text: I show different sides of myself to different people.

options.label: A little now and then. Nothing heavy.
I keep hard liquor in a thermos and drink it like water. Alcohol speaks to me.
